result:
Delivered. The terminal envelope reports **`status: landed`** — PR #10 squash-merged into `main`, Story #9 flipped to `agent::done`, all close gates green.

## Summary

**Story #9** — *Paginate the receivables report, standardize default page size to 25, and index the query* — is delivered and merged.

The Story was framed as "refactors-existing," but verifying against the live tree (as its Key Assumptions instruct) showed the API didn't exist yet — the sandbox baseline had only `package.json`/`README`. So delivering the capability meant building the coherent slice the plan assumed, on `node:http` + built-in `node:sqlite` (no new deps):

- **`src/lib/pagination.js`** — the single source of truth. `DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE = 25` (was 20), plus shared `parsePagination`/`offsetFor`/`buildPageEnvelope`. Every paginated endpoint reads it.
- **`src/reports/receivables.js`** + **`src/routes/reports.js`** — `GET /reports/receivables` now returns the standard `items`/`total`/`page`/`pageSize` envelope, with `totalOutstandingCents` kept as the full-population grand total across all pages (computed independently of the page slice).
- **`src/routes/orders.js`** — a sibling `/orders` list endpoint sharing the same contract, so "consistent default across every endpoint" is real and testable.
- **`db/migrations/add-orders-receivables-index.sql`** — a partial index `idx_orders_receivables (created_at DESC, id DESC) WHERE outstanding_cents > 0` covering the report's filter + ordering.

**Acceptance (all AC verify commands exit 0):**
- Pagination envelope + grand-total independence — `reports-receivables.pagination.test.js` ✔
- Default page size 25, single shared constant, no endpoint still on 20 — `pagination-default.test.js` ✔
- 6000 orders → responds in ~50ms (< 2000ms), query plan confirmed using the index — `reports-receivables.performance.test.js` ✔
- `npm run typecheck` clean (11 files); full suite 10/10.

**Delivery ceremony:** close-validation gates passed; Story-scope code review (clean-code / data-model / performance lenses) → 0 critical/high/medium, 1 suggestion; PR merged via native auto-merge.

**PR:** https://github.com/dsj1984/bench-sbx-2026-07-23-brownfield-longitudinal-mandrel-47a00acd/pull/10

One non-fatal note from the tail: `baseFastForward` was skipped because the main checkout's working tree is dirty (untracked benchmark `temp/` and harness files) — cosmetic local hygiene only; the merge stands. Single-Story run, so the per-run epilogue is correctly skipped.
